title: euphorbia hyberna subsp. euhyberna
intro: Euphorbia hyberna subsp. euhyberna, also known as Spurge or Irish Wolfspurge, is a perennial plant native to Ireland and the UK, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and milky sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Euphorbia hyberna subsp. euhyberna have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Euphorbia hyberna subsp. euhyberna is often prepared as a tincture or salve for topical application, and its sap can also be used as a natural remedy for various health issues.
